sections.title: Aspect Pair Patterns
sections.tip: Most aspect pairs are formed by adding a prefix: читать → прочитать. Some change suffix: покупать → купить. A few are completely different words: говорить → сказать.
sections.explanation: Most Russian verbs come in aspect pairs: imperfective (ongoing/repeated) and perfective (completed/single). Common patterns: add a prefix to make perfective: читать → прочитать, писать → написать, делать → сделать. Some pairs differ by suffix: покупать → купить, получать → получить.

exercises.prompt: What is the perfective pair of 'читать' (to read)?
exercises.explanation: Читать → прочитать (to read and finish). 'Зачитать' means to read aloud, 'перечитать' means to reread.

exercises.prompt: The perfective pair of 'писать' (to write) is ___.
exercises.explanation: Писать → написать. The prefix на- marks completion.

exercises.prompt: Match the pairs:
exercises.explanation: Each imperfective verb pairs with a perfective formed by adding a prefix: про-, на-, с-.

exercises.prompt: What is the perfective of 'готовить' (to cook/prepare)?
exercises.explanation: Готовить → приготовить (to prepare/cook completely). The prefix при- marks completion here.

exercises.prompt: The perfective pair of 'учить' (to learn/study) is ___.
exercises.explanation: Учить → выучить (to learn completely). The prefix вы- indicates thorough completion.

exercises.prompt: Form the perfective:
exercises.explanation: Строить → построить. Past tense plural: построили.

exercises.prompt: Which verb is imperfective?
exercises.explanation: 'Рисовать' (to draw/paint) is imperfective — no prefix. 'Нарисовать' and 'сделать' are perfective.

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error:
exercises.explanation: The correct perfective of 'писать' is 'написать' (prefix на-), not 'зписать'.

exercises.prompt: The perfective pair of 'покупать' (to buy) is ___.
exercises.hint: This pair changes the suffix, not adds a prefix
exercises.explanation: Покупать → купить. Some aspect pairs differ by suffix rather than prefix: -ать (imperf.) vs. -ить (perf.).

exercises.prompt: What is the imperfective pair of 'ответить' (to answer)?
exercises.explanation: Ответить (perf.) ↔ отвечать (imperf.). The suffix changes: -ить → -ать.

sections.title: Prefix Meanings
sections.tip: Different prefixes create new verbs with different meanings: на- (complete), пере- (re-/across), под- (sign/under), за- (record/begin), вы- (out).
sections.explanation: Adding different prefixes to the same verb creates new verbs with different meanings. For example: писать (to write) → написать (complete writing), переписать (rewrite), выписать (write out/extract), подписать (sign), записать (write down/record).

exercises.prompt: What does 'переписать' mean?
exercises.explanation: Prefix пере- means 're-' or 'across': переписать = to rewrite.

exercises.prompt: Пожалуйста, ___ это слово. (write down — записать)
exercises.explanation: Записать = to write down/record. Imperative: запиши (informal) / запишите (formal).

exercises.prompt: Match the pairs:
exercises.explanation: Each prefix changes the base meaning of 'писать': на- (complete), пере- (re-), под- (under/sign), за- (record).

exercises.prompt: Он ___ документ. (signed)
exercises.explanation: Подписать = to sign. Past tense masc.: подписал.

exercises.prompt: Я хочу ___ еду. (to cook, perfective — prefix при-)
exercises.explanation: При- + готовить = приготовить (to cook/prepare completely).

exercises.prompt: Add the correct prefix to change the meaning:
exercises.explanation: Вы- + идти = выйти (to go out). Past tense masc.: вышел.

exercises.prompt: What does 'перечитать' mean?
exercises.explanation: Prefix пере- means 're-': перечитать = to reread.

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error:
exercises.explanation: 'Выписать' means to write out/extract. To sign a contract is 'подписать': подписала контракт.

exercises.prompt: Мне нужно ___ этот текст. (to rewrite — пере-)
exercises.explanation: Переписать = to rewrite. Prefix пере- means 're-' or 'again'.

exercises.prompt: What does prefix 'до-' usually mean in 'дочитать'?
exercises.explanation: Prefix до- means 'to the end' or 'up to': дочитать = to finish reading (to the end).

sections.title: Aspect in Past Tense
sections.tip: Imperfective past = was doing, used to do (process). Perfective past = did, finished doing (result). Он читал книгу (was reading) vs Он прочитал книгу (finished reading).
sections.explanation: In the past tense, imperfective verbs express ongoing, repeated, or habitual actions (was doing, used to do). Perfective verbs express completed, one-time actions (did, finished). Он читал книгу (was reading) vs. Он прочитал книгу (finished reading).

examples.en: He was reading a book all evening. (imperfective)

examples.ru: Он прочитал книгу за два дня.
examples.en: He read (finished) the book in two days. (perfective)

exercises.prompt: Она ___ письмо целый час. (was writing — ongoing)
exercises.explanation: Imperfective 'писала' shows ongoing action over a period of time (целый час = for a whole hour).

exercises.prompt: Он наконец ___ эту книгу. (finished reading — perfective past masc.)
exercises.explanation: 'Наконец' (finally) signals completion, so perfective 'прочитал' is correct.

exercises.prompt: Какое предложение описывает завершённое действие? (Which describes a completed action?)
exercises.explanation: 'Сделал' (perfective past) = completed. 'Делал' (imperfective past) = was doing.

exercises.prompt: Rewrite with a perfective verb to show completion:
exercises.explanation: Готовила (was cooking) → приготовила (cooked/finished cooking).

exercises.prompt: В детстве я часто ___ в парке. (used to play — imperfective past)
exercises.explanation: 'Часто' (often) + childhood memory = habitual action, requiring imperfective 'играл/играла'.

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error:
exercises.incorrectText: Я уже написал это письмо три раза вчера.
exercises.correctedText: Я писал это письмо три раза вчера.
exercises.explanation: 'Три раза' (three times) implies repetition, which requires imperfective 'писал'. Perfective 'написал' would mean completed once.

exercises.prompt: Я ___ кофе и пошёл на работу. (drank and left)
exercises.explanation: Two sequential completed actions: выпил (drank up, perfective) and пошёл (left, perfective).

exercises.prompt: Match the pairs:
exercises.explanation: Imperfective past = process/ongoing. Perfective past = completed result.

exercises.prompt: Когда я ___, позвонил друг. (was eating — imperfective past masc.)
exercises.explanation: The background action (was eating) is imperfective: ел. The interrupting action (called) is perfective.

exercises.prompt: Arrange into a correct sentence:
exercises.translation: She cooked dinner and we ate.
exercises.explanation: Both verbs are perfective (приготовила, поели) because both actions were completed in sequence.

sections.title: Choosing Aspect in Context
sections.tip: Signal words: часто/всегда/долго/каждый день → imperfective. Уже/наконец/вдруг/сразу → perfective.
sections.explanation: Choosing the right aspect depends on context. Use imperfective for: ongoing actions, habits, duration, general statements. Use perfective for: completed actions, results, single events, sequences. Key words: imperfective — часто, всегда, долго, каждый день. Perfective — уже, наконец, вдруг, сразу.

examples.en: I always have breakfast at eight. (habitual = imperf.)
She was choosing a gift for a long time. (duration = imperf.)

exercises.prompt: Я ___ эту книгу каждый вечер. (read — every evening)
exercises.explanation: 'Каждый вечер' (every evening) indicates a habit, requiring imperfective 'читаю'.

exercises.prompt: Он вдруг ___ и вышел. (stood up — perfective past masc.)
exercises.explanation: 'Вдруг' (suddenly) signals a single, abrupt action — perfective: встал (stood up).

exercises.prompt: Ты уже ___ домашнее задание? (Did you finish your homework?)
exercises.explanation: 'Уже' (already) implies a result/completion — perfective 'сделал'.

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct aspect:
exercises.context: Я долго ___ эту тему, но наконец ___.
exercises.hint: First blank: ongoing process. Second: completed result.
exercises.explanation: 'Долго' = duration (imperfective учил). 'Наконец' = completion (perfective выучил).

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error:
exercises.incorrectText: Она всегда приготовила обед в шесть.
exercises.correctedText: Она всегда готовила обед в шесть.
exercises.explanation: 'Всегда' (always) indicates a habit, requiring imperfective 'готовила', not perfective 'приготовила'.

exercises.prompt: Я ___ тебе завтра. (will call — perfective future)
exercises.explanation: A single planned future action uses perfective: позвоню (I will call).

exercises.prompt: Когда я ___ улицу, начался дождь. (was crossing)
exercises.explanation: Background action interrupted by another event = imperfective: переходил (was crossing).

exercises.prompt: Match the pairs:
exercises.explanation: Signal words help determine aspect: duration/habit = imperfective, completion/result = perfective.

exercises.prompt: Не ___ меня! Я занят. (disturb — imperfective imperative)
exercises.explanation: Prohibitions and general requests typically use imperfective: Не беспокой! (Don't disturb!)

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear:
exercises.audio: Я долго писал письмо, но наконец написал.
acceptedAnswers: Я долго писал письмо, но наконец написал.
Я долго писал письмо, но наконец написал
exercises.explanation: 'Долго писал' (imperfective = was writing for a long time) contrasts with 'наконец написал' (perfective = finally finished writing).
